description: tty-theme v1.3 — Web-only pivot. No CLI. Server-side Gemini+Groq keys, rate limiting (5/day + exponential backoff), prompt injection protection, community gallery with download counts.

userStories.title: Remove CLI binary and all CLI code
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to delete cli/ directory, modes/image_mode.py, image/ directory, and remove CLI-related deps from pyproject.toml so the codebase is web-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete cli/ directory entirely
Delete modes/ directory entirely (image_mode.py and prompt_mode.py become part of the API layer)
Delete image/ directory (loader.py, extractor.py, phash.py, mapper.py) if it exists
Remove typer, keyring from pyproject.toml dependencies
Remove [project.scripts] tty-theme entry point from pyproject.toml
Remove test_image_mode.py, test_image_loader.py, test_cli.py from tests/ if they exist
uv run pytest passes (pre-existing failures in test_llm.py and test_api.py are acceptable)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: v1.3 is web-only. Clean removal only — no replacement needed in this story.

userStories.title: Add structured prompt template with injection protection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need generator/prompt.py to wrap user input in a structural system+user prompt so injected instructions cannot affect LLM behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Create generator/prompt.py with SYSTEM_PROMPT constant
SYSTEM_PROMPT instructs LLM to output ONLY JSON with exactly 21 hex keys, ignore any embedded instructions
Add build_user_prompt(raw_input: str) -> str that wraps raw_input as: 'Generate a terminal color theme inspired by: "{raw_input}"'
build_user_prompt does NOT modify or filter the input — sanitization is upstream in input_sanitizer.py
Tests: build_user_prompt('cyberpunk neon rain') produces a string containing the raw input wrapped in quotes
Tests: SYSTEM_PROMPT contains the words 'ignore' and 'JSON' (confirming injection-defense language is present)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Security foundation — must exist before any LLM call is made.

userStories.title: Enforce 200-char max and sanitize prompt input
userStories.description: As a developer, I need security/input_sanitizer.py to enforce a 200-character limit and strip control characters so user input is safe before wrapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sanitize_prompt(raw: str) -> str to security/input_sanitizer.py (or update existing sanitize function)
Raises ValueError('prompt_too_long') if len(raw.strip()) > 200
Strips leading/trailing whitespace
Normalizes unicode to NFKC
Strips null bytes and ASCII control characters (\x00-\x1f, \x7f)
Does NOT further filter content — structural wrapping handles injection
Tests: input of 201 chars raises ValueError
Tests: input with null bytes has them stripped
Tests: unicode input is NFKC normalized
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Max 200 chars enforced here AND at API level (belt-and-suspenders).

userStories.title: Add Gemini + Groq providers via OpenAICompatProvider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need providers/openai_compat.py and providers/registry.py to support Gemini 2.0 Flash and Groq as the two server-side providers with 429 auto-fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: providers/openai_compat.py has OpenAICompatProvider class with CATALOGUE dict containing 'gemini' and 'groq' entries
CATALOGUE['gemini']: base_url='https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai', model='gemini-2.0-flash', key_env='GEMINI_API_KEY'
CATALOGUE['groq']: base_url='https://api.groq.com/openai', model='llama-3.3-70b-versatile', key_env='GROQ_API_KEY'
providers/registry.py has generate_with_fallback(prompt: str, system: str) -> str that tries gemini first, then groq on HTTP 429
Non-429 errors propagate immediately
Tests: mock HTTP 429 from gemini → asserts groq was called
Tests: mock non-429 error from gemini → asserts error propagates, groq not called
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Server-side keys only. GEMINI_API_KEY and GROQ_API_KEY read from env.

userStories.title: Add slug generation utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared make_slug() function for consistent URL-safe theme slugs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add make_slug(name: str) -> str in generator/slug.py
Lowercases, replaces spaces/underscores with hyphens, strips non-alphanumeric chars except hyphens
Collapses consecutive hyphens, strips leading/trailing hyphens
make_slug('Tokyo Midnight!') == 'tokyo-midnight'
make_slug('  --neon rain-- ') == 'neon-rain'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Used by publish and gallery API endpoints.

userStories.title: Add per-IP rate limiting (5/day + burst cooldown) to API middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I need api/middleware.py to enforce 5 LLM generations per IP per day and exponential backoff on bursting, stored in the theme repository.
acceptanceCriteria: Add check_rate_limit(ip_hash: str, repo) -> None to api/middleware.py or a new api/rate_limit.py
Raises RateLimitExceeded(reset_at: datetime) if daily count >= DAILY_GENERATION_LIMIT (env, default 5)
Raises BurstCooldown(cooldown_seconds: int) if IP made > 3 requests within BURST_WINDOW_SECONDS (env, default 60)
Burst cooldowns: 1st=10s, 2nd=30s, 3rd+=120s
IP stored as SHA256(ip)[:16] — never raw IP
increment_rate_limit(ip_hash: str, repo) -> None increments count and records request timestamp
Rate limit state stored in SQLite (dev) / Firestore (prod) via a rate_limits table/collection
Cache hits (exact/similarity) do NOT call increment_rate_limit
Tests: 5 calls succeed, 6th raises RateLimitExceeded
Tests: 4 calls within 60s triggers BurstCooldown on 4th
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Rate limits only apply to actual LLM calls, not cache hits.

userStories.title: Add rate_limits table to SQLite repository
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cache/db.py to store and query per-IP rate limit state so the middleware can persist limits across requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Add rate_limits table: ip_hash TEXT PK, daily_count INT, day TEXT (YYYY-MM-DD), burst_timestamps TEXT (JSON array of ISO strings), burst_offense_count INT
Add get_rate_limit(ip_hash) -> dict | None to ThemeRepository
Add upsert_rate_limit(ip_hash, daily_count, day, burst_timestamps, burst_offense_count) -> None
day column resets count logic: if stored day != today, treat count as 0
Tests: upsert then get returns correct values
Tests: day mismatch returns count=0
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must exist before US-006 middleware can store state.

userStories.title: Add POST /v1/generate endpoint
userStories.description: As a web user, I want to POST a prompt and receive a generated theme with full rate limiting and injection protection applied.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /v1/generate to api/main.py
Request body: { prompt: str }
Returns 400 { error: 'prompt_too_long', max_length: 200 } if prompt > 200 chars
Calls sanitize_prompt() then build_user_prompt() then generate_with_fallback()
Checks rate limit before LLM call; returns 429 { error: 'rate_limit_exceeded', reset_at, cooldown_seconds } if exceeded
Increments rate limit counter after successful LLM call only
Returns 200 { theme_data, slug, cached, provider }
theme_data is Ghostty key=value format
slug is make_slug(prompt[:40])
cached: true if served from Tier 1 or Tier 2 cache
Tests: 201-char prompt returns 400
Tests: 6th request returns 429
Tests: valid prompt returns 200 with theme_data
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Core endpoint. Wires together all previous stories.

userStories.title: Add GET /v1/themes and GET /v1/themes/:slug endpoints
userStories.description: As a web gallery user, I want to browse and fetch community themes via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /v1/themes to api/main.py: params sort (newest|downloads, default newest), limit (max 100, default 20), offset
Returns { themes: [{id, slug, name, download_count, created_at, theme_data}], total, offset }
Add GET /v1/themes/{slug} to api/main.py
Returns theme object or 404 { error: 'not_found' }
slug matching uses make_slug(name) == slug
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add POST /v1/themes and POST /v1/themes/:slug/download endpoints
userStories.description: As a web user, I want to publish a theme to the gallery and track download counts.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /v1/themes: body { name: str, theme_data: str }
Validates theme_data with validate_theme() — returns 422 on failure
Generates slug with make_slug(name)
Saves with source='community', download_count=0
Returns 201 { slug, name, created_at } or 409 if slug exists
Add POST /v1/themes/{slug}/download: increments download_count by 1
Returns 200 { download_count: N } or 404 if slug not found
Add download_count column (INT default 0) to themes table in cache/db.py
Add increment_download_count(slug) -> int to ThemeRepository
Tests: publish returns 201 with slug
Tests: duplicate slug returns 409
Tests: download endpoint increments count
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PRD §9. download_count must be added to DB schema.

userStories.title: Add GET /v1/neofetch/:slug endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a text/plain ANSI color block for any theme for terminal README embeds.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /v1/neofetch/{slug} to api/main.py returning text/plain
Parses theme_data key=value to extract palette colors 0-15 plus background/foreground
Outputs theme name on first line, then two rows of 8 colored blocks using ANSI \x1b[48;2;R;G;Bm  \x1b[0m
Returns 404 plain text 'Theme not found' if slug not found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build generator page (web/index.html)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to visit tty-theme.dev, type a prompt, and download a theme — all from the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Create web/index.html with a prompt textarea (maxlength=200), live character counter '0 / 200', and Generate button
Generate button calls POST /v1/generate via fetch(); shows spinner while loading
On success: renders 16-color swatch strip (colored divs) and Download Ghostty + Download iTerm2 buttons
Download Ghostty: serializes theme_data as-is, triggers file download as <slug>.ghostty, then calls POST /v1/themes/:slug/download

Download iTerm2: calls GET /v1/themes/:slug to get theme_data, serializes via iterm2 format client-side OR requests ?format=iterm2 from API, triggers file download, then calls POST /v1/themes/:slug/download
On 429 rate_limit_exceeded: shows 'Daily limit reached. Resets in HH:MM:SS' with live countdown to reset_at
On 429 with cooldown_seconds > 0: disables Generate button, shows live countdown
On 400 prompt_too_long: shows inline error (should be caught by maxlength but handle anyway)
Publish button: shows name input, calls POST /v1/themes, shows shareable URL on success
Uses CDN Tailwind for styling
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: PRD §10. Zero build step — vanilla JS + CDN Tailwind.

userStories.title: Build gallery page (web/gallery.html)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse community themes at /gallery.
acceptanceCriteria: Create web/gallery.html that calls GET /v1/themes on load
Renders theme cards: name, 8-color swatch strip, download_count badge, 'Install: tty-theme install <slug>' copy chip
Sort toggle: Newest | Most Downloaded (re-fetches with sort param)
Clicking a card links to /theme.html?slug=<slug>
Empty state: 'No themes yet. Be the first to generate and publish one!'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build theme detail page (web/theme.html)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a shareable page for each theme at /theme.html?slug=<slug>.
acceptanceCriteria: Create web/theme.html that reads ?slug= from URL, calls GET /v1/themes/:slug
Shows theme name, full 16-color palette swatch strip
Shows download_count badge
Download Ghostty and Download iTerm2 buttons (same logic as index.html, increments count)
Shows neofetch ANSI block preview (calls GET /v1/neofetch/:slug, displays in <pre>)
404 state: 'Theme not found'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update mockup.html to reflect v1.3 web-only design
userStories.description: As a stakeholder, I want mockup.html to show the v1.3 web UI: no CLI references, prompt input with char counter, gallery with download counts.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove all CLI-related content (install commands, tty-theme binary references) from mockup.html
Remove image tab and image upload UI
Show prompt textarea with maxlength=200 and live character counter
Show rate limit UX section: '5 generations/day — Daily limit reached' countdown example
Show gallery section with download_count badges on theme cards
Typecheck passes (HTML/JS only)
userStories.notes: CLAUDE.md: mockup.html stays in sync with PRD.
